stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The international Fair Trade movement was founded in 1988 with the launch of the Max Havelaar certification mark in the Netherlands.
  • The movement promotes guaranteed minimum prices and stable contracts for smallholder farmers.
  • It calls for environmentally responsible production practices that protect soil, water, and biodiversity on participating farms.
  • It advocates for democratic cooperative governance among producer organizations.
  • The Fair Trade USA organization was founded in 1998.
stem: The student wants to introduce the Fair Trade movement to a new audience. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The launch of the Max Havelaar certification mark in the Netherlands in 1988 marked the founding of the international Fair Trade movement, while the Fair Trade USA organization was founded in 1998.
The Fair Trade movement advocates for smallholder farmers.
Goals of the movement include democratic cooperative governance and environmentally responsible production practices.

The international Fair Trade movement, founded in 1988, promotes guaranteed minimum prices and stable contracts for smallholder farmers through environmentally responsible production practices and democratic cooperative governance.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence effectively introduces the Fair Trade movement to a new audience, explaining that the movement, founded in 1988, promotes minimum prices and stable contracts for smallholder farmers through environmentally responsible practices and democratic cooperative governance.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence indicates when the international Fair Trade movement and Fair Trade USA were founded, it does not effectively introduce the movement to a new audience. Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence notes that the Fair Trade movement advocates for smallholder farmers, it does not effectively introduce the movement. Choice C is incorrect. While the sentence describes some of the goals of the Fair Trade movement, it does not effectively introduce the movement to a new audience.
